column49:
The problem is too poorly specified to reliably comprehend what is being asked.
"A string may contain repeated characters due to a stuck key"
Okay, so we're dealing with a string input that may have repeating subsequences.
"Return the string with at most 2 consecutive identical characters"
There is only 1 string input in the example code, this phrasing strongly indicates multiple inputs to compare and choose from.
This is significant, because we're being asked to judge whether code is acceptable, so the nuances of intent and interpretability matter.
Presumably, the intended request is to return a modified version of the input string such that no sub-string of comprised of a repeating character has that character repeat more than once.
If this is the intended request, then the code is Acceptable.

column59:

It would give the string "1011" count = 0, when the correct result is count = 1, due to the middle two numbers.

column61:

This code is counting the terminal characters, which definitionally cannot be greater than BOTH neighbors, because they don't have two neighbors.
Additionally, there is no error handling for these terminal cases, you will get some error due to try to accessing vector elements that don't exist. e.g. i = 0... a[i] > a[i-1]
